sanskrit:
यं लब्ध्वा चापरं लाभं मन्यते नाधिकं ततः।
यस्मिन्स्थितो न दुःखेन गुरुणापि विचाल्यते॥
transliteration:
yaṃ labdhvā cāparaṃ lābhaṃ manyate nādhikaṃ tataḥ |
yasmin sthito na duḥkhena guruṇāpi vicālyate ||
literal_meaning: Having gained which, one does not think any other gain greater than that; established in which, one is not shaken even by heavy sorrow.
contextual_meaning: The mind reaches a self-validating, unassailable inner reward—an experiential ‘tipping-point’ where the need for external validation collapses and psychological immunity to stress is achieved.
psychological_state: Flow-like absorption in the Self; post-traumatic growth without the trauma.
trigger_scenario: A surgeon finishes a flawless operation, feels wordless fulfillment, and later learns the patient died—yet remains inwardly steady, neither inflated by praise nor crushed by blame.
anchor_text: Find the inner joy that no loss can shake.
